name: ifm-practitioners
description: Search IFM's public practitioner directory by location and extract listing-card contact fields from server-rendered HTML, with optional full-detail enrichment and browser fallback for challenge cases.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.note: auto tries direct HTTP first, then browser fallback. Use http or browser to force a runtime.

inputs.name: lat
inputs.note: Optional manual latitude to skip geocoding.

inputs.name: lon
inputs.note: Optional manual longitude to skip geocoding.

inputs.name: proxy
inputs.note: Optional residential proxy URL. Do not commit the value.

inputs.name: ignore_https_errors
inputs.note: Useful for SSL-inspection residential proxies when their CA is not installed in the browser profile.

inputs.name: http_retries
inputs.note: Retries transient direct HTTP Cloudflare challenge/403 responses before failing or falling back in auto mode.

inputs.name: request_delay
inputs.note: Sleep seconds between direct HTTP page/detail requests to reduce challenge risk during bulk runs.

auth.note: Uses public IFM directory pages through direct HTTP. Browser fallback may need one headed Cloudflare verification before unattended reuse.

verification_result: Direct HTTP listing run returned 10 records with 153 total available in 4.72s. Direct HTTP detail run returned 3 enriched profiles in 14.62s with email coverage for all 3 records; the first profile included 10 health concerns and 7 coursework values. Cookie-backed replay was not needed; IFM cookies made the tested replay return Cloudflare challenge responses.
known_risks: IFM uses Cloudflare managed challenge and direct HTTP can intermittently return 403/challenge responses.
The script retries direct HTTP and auto mode falls back to browser DOM extraction when direct HTTP fails.
Residential proxy support is useful for reachability but did not guarantee fresh-session access in the tested IFM browser run.
Use --headed for first-run browser fallback verification, RESIDENTIAL_PROXY_URL where allowed, and --ignore-https-errors for SSL-inspection proxies unless the proxy CA is installed.
This is HTML extraction from public listing/detail pages, not an official public JSON API.
